articles.title: Raw Thrills Launches Stranger Things Arcade Redemption Game
articles.summary: Raw Thrills has globally launched Stranger Things Arcade, a 4-player family redemption game set across iconic locations from the hit Netflix series. The title targets FEC operators looking for high-profile licensed IP to drive repeat visits and redemption revenue. Stranger Things remains one of the most-watched series ever, giving operators a strong marketing hook.

articles.title: Disney Outlines AI Plans for Labor Forecasting and Guest Personalization
articles.summary: Disney's new CEO Josh D'Amaro revealed the company is deploying AI to personalize trip planning and optimize labor demand forecasting across its theme parks. For FEC operators, the strategy offers a roadmap for how AI-driven staffing and personalized booking tools could reduce costs and improve the guest experience. Disney's scale makes it an early signal of where the broader attractions industry is heading.

articles.title: Foundations Entertainment University Schedules Tennessee Education Event
articles.summary: Foundations Entertainment University, a leading training program for FEC operators, has announced an upcoming event in Tennessee. The program covers business planning, design, operations, and revenue strategy specifically tailored to the FEC industry. Operators considering new builds or expansions should note the event as a valuable networking and education opportunity.

articles.title: Florida Settles for Undisclosed Amount After 6-Year-Old Killed in Go-Kart Crash
articles.summary: A settlement has been reached between the state of Florida and the family of a 6-year-old child who died in a go-kart crash at an entertainment venue. The case is a critical reminder for FEC operators running go-kart attractions to review safety protocols, age and height restrictions, and liability coverage. Incidents like this can have significant legal and reputational consequences for the industry.

articles.title: Amusement Center to Pay $300K Settlement Following Child's Death Investigation
articles.summary: An amusement center has agreed to a $300,000 settlement following an investigation into the death of a young girl at the facility. The case underscores the ongoing legal and safety risks facing FEC operators and the importance of rigorous incident reporting and compliance. Operators should use this as a prompt to audit safety procedures and ensure adequate insurance coverage.

articles.title: WhiteWater Highlights 60+ Waterpark Project Openings Worldwide in 2026
articles.summary: WhiteWater, the world's largest water attraction manufacturer, is celebrating over 60 project openings globally this year spanning waterparks, FlowRider surf simulators, and wave pool installations. Highlights include the record-breaking Aquarabia park in Riyadh and multiple projects across North America and Europe. The surge in openings signals strong capital investment in aquatic FEC-adjacent attractions and growing operator demand for innovative water experiences.

articles.title: New Family Entertainment Center Planned for Mt. Juliet, Tennessee
articles.summary: A new family entertainment center is coming to Mt. Juliet, Tennessee, adding to the growing pipeline of FEC development in suburban markets. The project reflects continued investor and operator confidence in FEC concepts as community leisure destinations. Details on the concept and operator have not yet been disclosed.

articles.title: 'Skate Central' Family Entertainment Center Coming to Beckley, West Virginia
articles.summary: A new FEC called Skate Central is set to open in Beckley, West Virginia, bringing skating and family entertainment to the market. The development highlights continued growth of FEC concepts in smaller and mid-sized markets. Skating-anchored FECs have seen a resurgence as operators look to differentiate with activity-based experiences.

articles.title: Chuck E. Cheese Named Among USA Today's Most Trusted Brands by Parents
articles.summary: Chuck E. Cheese has been recognized by USA Today as one of the most trusted brands by parents, a significant endorsement for the long-running FEC chain. Brand trust is a key driver of repeat visitation in the family entertainment segment, and this recognition could support franchise and marketing efforts. The accolade comes as Chuck E. Cheese continues its transformation toward a more modern FEC format.

articles.title: New 'Gameland' Venue Opens in Cookstown with VR, Sim Racing, and Retro Gaming
articles.summary: A new family entertainment venue called Gameland has opened in Cookstown, Northern Ireland, offering a mix of VR experiences, sim racing, and retro arcade gaming. The multi-concept format reflects the trend of FECs blending classic and cutting-edge technology to broaden their guest appeal. Operators can look to this model as an example of diversifying revenue streams within a single venue footprint.

articles.title: Black Bunny FEC Combines Gaming, Adventure, and Family Entertainment in Dubai
articles.summary: Black Bunny, a new family entertainment concept in Dubai, is blending gaming, adventure activities, and social entertainment into a single venue. The Middle East continues to be a hotbed for new FEC investment, driven by high consumer spending and government tourism initiatives. The concept offers design inspiration for operators seeking to create immersive, multi-activity destinations.

articles.title: 23 Jump Street Trampoline and Adventure Venue Opens at Rivergate, Cape Town
articles.summary: 23 Jump Street has opened a new high-energy family entertainment venue at the Rivergate retail development in Cape Town, South Africa. The opening is part of a broader trend of FEC concepts anchoring retail and mixed-use developments internationally. Active entertainment formats like trampoline parks remain popular draws for shopping centers looking to increase dwell time.

articles.title: Claw Arcade Facility Heads to City Planning Panel for Approval
articles.summary: A claw arcade facility concept is going before a city planning panel, reflecting the growing wave of specialized single-concept arcade venues seeking municipal approval. Claw machines have surged in popularity, and standalone claw arcade venues are emerging as a new FEC sub-category. Operators considering similar concepts should monitor permitting and zoning considerations in their markets.

articles.title: Semantic's LOOP Platform Enables Multi-Brand Website Management for Attraction Operators
articles.summary: Semantic has launched LOOP, a centralized CMS platform that allows attraction operators to manage multiple brand websites and locations from a single system. The platform is designed to reduce digital fragmentation while keeping booking, security, and performance functions unified. For FEC operators with multiple locations or sub-brands, LOOP could significantly streamline digital marketing and reduce third-party web development costs.

articles.title: Ellis & Associates Launches AI-Supported Drowning Detection eLearning Course
articles.summary: Ellis & Associates has released a new AI-supported online training course focused on scanning techniques and real-time drowning detection for aquatic safety professionals. The course uses real video footage of swimmers in distress to improve lifeguard recognition skills. FEC operators running waterparks, pools, or aquatic attractions should consider the course as part of their staff safety training programs.

articles.title: P2:3 Consulting Partners with Aqua Tile to Improve Waterpark Surface Safety
articles.summary: P2:3 Consulting has announced a partnership with Aqua Tile to help waterpark operators upgrade wet-area surfaces for improved safety, guest comfort, and easier maintenance. The collaboration targets aging infrastructure challenges that are common across aquatic FECs. Operators dealing with slip hazards or surface replacement projects may find the partnership's solutions worth evaluating.

articles.title: Story Land Reveals Full Lineup of New Experiences for 2026 Season
articles.summary: Story Land in Glen, New Hampshire, is launching an all-new slate of shows, reimagined attractions, and programming updates for its 2026 season. The park's strategy of refreshing the guest experience annually with new entertainment formats is a solid model for FEC operators looking to drive repeat visitation. New shows and activity-based experiences are increasingly important differentiators in competitive family entertainment markets.

articles.title: Adventureland Iowa Opens May 16 with New Events and Enhanced Food Offerings
articles.summary: Adventureland in Altoona, Iowa, is set to open for its 2026 season on May 16, featuring new seasonal events and expanded dining options alongside returning rides and entertainment. Enhanced F&B is an increasingly important revenue driver for FEC and amusement park operators competing for the leisure dollar. The park's seasonal programming approach provides a template for operators planning their own calendar of events.

articles.title: Monster Mini Golf Location in Coral Springs Closes
articles.summary: The Monster Mini Golf franchise location in Coral Springs, Florida has shut down this week, marking another closure in the indoor mini golf segment. Closures of established franchise locations are a useful signal for operators to assess local market saturation and the ongoing viability of single-concept FEC formats. The indoor mini golf segment continues to evolve as consumers demand more diverse entertainment options.

articles.title: Former Queensbury Amusement Center Eyed for Conversion to Distillery
articles.summary: A former amusement center in Queensbury, New York is being considered for conversion into a distillery, illustrating the challenge of repurposing FEC real estate when venues close. The case highlights adaptive reuse trends affecting entertainment properties and serves as a reminder for operators about lease terms and exit strategies. It also points to the competitive pressure FECs face from other leisure and hospitality concepts.

articles.title: India Emerges as the Next Major Market for Attractions and Entertainment
articles.summary: India's rapidly growing middle class of 500 million people, expected to increase by 310 million over the next decade, is positioned to become the world's next major market for entertainment and recreation. With 66% of the population under 35 and rising consumer spending on leisure, the country presents significant expansion opportunities for FEC operators and attraction developers. Industry experts note strong demand for entertainment-led design across small, mid, and large-scale projects.

articles.title: Saudi Arabia's Tourism Economy Grows 7.4%, Driving Middle East Attraction Investment
articles.summary: Saudi Arabia's travel and tourism GDP rose 7.4% in 2025 to $178 billion, making it the largest tourism market in the Middle East and a growing destination for attractions investment. International visitor spending in the kingdom grew 8.2%, nearly triple the global average, signaling robust demand for new FEC and entertainment concepts. Operators and suppliers targeting international expansion should take note of the kingdom's accelerating development pipeline.

articles.title: Six Flags Announces Leadership Transitions
articles.summary: Six Flags has announced leadership changes within its executive team, a development worth tracking for suppliers and partners who work closely with the major regional theme park operator. Leadership transitions at large operators often signal shifts in strategic priorities around capital investment, technology, and vendor relationships. FEC industry stakeholders should monitor how new leadership may influence the company's direction on expansion and guest experience.

articles.title: Disneyland Moves Toward Cashless Operations
articles.summary: Disneyland is preparing for a cashless future, moving toward an all-digital payments environment across its parks. For FEC operators, Disney's cashless pivot is an influential signal given the brand's market-setting role, and it may accelerate adoption of cashless payment systems across the broader attractions industry. Operators still relying on cash transactions should consider the operational and guest experience benefits of transitioning to card and mobile payments.

articles.title: MSC World Asia Cruise Ship Debuts Open-Air Theme Park Onboard
articles.summary: MSC Cruises has launched an open-air theme park aboard its new flagship MSC World Asia, bringing amusement-style attractions to the cruise industry. The development is relevant to FEC operators and attraction suppliers as cruise lines increasingly compete for the same entertainment-focused leisure spending. It also signals growing demand for turnkey attraction packages in non-traditional venue formats.

articles.title: Zamperla Delivers First Family Launch Coaster to Parc Spirou in France
articles.summary: Zamperla has supplied its first Family Launch Coaster, themed around the Naruto manga series, to Parc Spirou in France as part of a new 15,000 sq meter themed zone. The installation also includes a Nebulaz ride, demonstrating the appeal of IP-driven ride packages that can generate over 1,000 guests per hour across a new area. FEC operators considering coaster or major ride additions can look to Zamperla's integrated rides approach as a capacity and theming model.

articles.title: SeaWorld Orlando Launches 'Electric Ocean' Summer Nighttime Event Starting June 12
articles.summary: SeaWorld Orlando is launching Electric Ocean, a premier summer nighttime event featuring light environments, music, and the return of nighttime animal shows beginning June 12. Seasonal nighttime events have proven effective at extending guest dwell time and increasing per-cap spending, a strategy applicable to FECs of all sizes. Operators planning summer programming can draw inspiration from SeaWorld's event-layered approach.

articles.title: Renewable Lubricants Marks 35 Years of Eco-Friendly Products for Amusement Industry
articles.summary: Renewable Lubricants is celebrating 35 years of manufacturing environmentally friendly and food-grade bio-based lubricants, with products serving the amusement and attractions industry among others. As sustainability becomes a growing priority for FEC operators, bio-based lubricants offer a way to reduce environmental impact in ride and equipment maintenance. The milestone highlights a long-standing supplier relationship with the attractions sector.

articles.title: Alabama Adventure & Splash Adventure Opens 2026 Season on May 9
articles.summary: Alabama Adventure and its adjacent Splash Adventure waterpark opened their gates for the 2026 season on May 9 in Bessemer, Alabama, welcoming families back to roller coasters, water slides, and wave pools. The combined amusement and waterpark model continues to demonstrate strong family appeal in regional markets. Seasonal openings like this signal the start of the peak summer revenue period for FEC operators across the country.
